API Reference¶

news.models¶

class news.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Model

A news article written by a journalist or editor.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

approved¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

approved_at¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

approved_by¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

approved_by_id¶
author¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

author_id¶
content¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

created_at¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_next_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=True, **kwargs)¶
get_next_by_updated_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: updated_at>, is_next=True, **kwargs)¶
get_previous_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)¶
get_previous_by_updated_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: updated_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)¶
id¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

newsletters¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>¶
publisher¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

publisher_id¶
title¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

updated_at¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

class news.models.CustomUser(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: AbstractUser

Custom user model supporting Reader, Journalist, and Editor roles. Role-specific fields are populated based on the assigned role; others are None.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

class Role(value)[source]¶

Bases: TextChoices

An enumeration.

EDITOR = 'editor'¶
JOURNALIST = 'journalist'¶
READER = 'reader'¶
approved_articles¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

authored_articles¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

save(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Save the current instance. Override this in a subclass if you want to control the saving process.

The ‘force_insert’ and ‘force_update’ parameters can be used to insist that the “save” must be an SQL insert or update (or equivalent for non-SQL backends), respectively. Normally, they should not be set.

subscribed_journalists¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

class news.models.Newsletter(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Model

A curated collection of articles assembled by a journalist or editor.

website¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

news.views¶

Views for the News Application.

Contains:

  • Template-based views for the web UI (article list, detail, approval dashboard).

  • Django REST Framework API views for the RESTful API.

class news.views.ApprovalDashboardView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: EditorRequiredMixin, ListView

Editors see all pending (unapproved) articles here.

context_object_name = 'articles'¶
model¶

alias of Article

queryset = <QuerySet []>¶
template_name = 'news/approval_dashboard.html'¶
class news.views.ArticleApprovalAPIView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: APIView

POST /api/articles/<id>/approve/ — approve an article (editors only).

permission_classes = [<class 'news.permissions.IsEditor'>]¶
post(request, pk)[source]¶
class news.views.ArticleCreateView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: JournalistOrEditorMixin, CreateView

Allow journalists and editors to submit a new article for review.

fields = ['title', 'content', 'publisher']¶
form_valid(form)[source]¶

If the form is valid, save the associated model.

model¶

alias of Article

success_url = '/'¶
template_name = 'news/article_form.html'¶
class news.views.ArticleDeleteView(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: JournalistOrEditorMixin, DeleteView

Allow an article’s author or any editor to delete an article.

get_queryset()[source]¶

Return the QuerySet that will be used to look up the object.

This method is called by the default implementation of get_object() and may not be called if get_object() is overridden.

model¶

alias of Article

success_url = '/'¶
template_name = 'news/article_confirm_delete.html'¶
class news.views.ArticleDetailView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, DetailView

Display a single article; readers are restricted to approved articles.

context_object_name = 'article'¶
get_queryset()[source]¶

Return the QuerySet that will be used to look up the object.

This method is called by the default implementation of get_object() and may not be called if get_object() is overridden.

model¶

alias of Article

template_name = 'news/article_detail.html'¶
class news.views.ArticleListCreateAPIView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ListCreateAPIView

GET /api/articles/ — list all approved articles (any authenticated user). POST /api/articles/ — create a new article (journalists only).

get_permissions()[source]¶

Instantiates and returns the list of permissions that this view requires.

get_queryset()[source]¶

Get the list of items for this view. This must be an iterable, and may be a queryset. Defaults to using self.queryset.

This method should always be used rather than accessing self.queryset directly, as self.queryset gets evaluated only once, and those results are cached for all subsequent requests.

You may want to override this if you need to provide different querysets depending on the incoming request.

(Eg. return a list of items that is specific to the user)

serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

class news.views.ArticleListView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, ListView

List articles; editors and journalists see all, readers see only approved ones.

context_object_name = 'articles'¶
get_queryset()[source]¶

Return the list of items for this view.

The return value must be an iterable and may be an instance of QuerySet in which case QuerySet specific behavior will be enabled.

model¶

alias of Article

template_name = 'news/article_list.html'¶
class news.views.ArticleRetrieveUpdateDestroyAPIView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: RetrieveUpdateDestroyAPIView

GET /api/articles/<id>/ — retrieve an article. PUT /api/articles/<id>/ — update (editors or article author). DELETE /api/articles/<id>/ — delete (editors or article author).

get_permissions()[source]¶

Instantiates and returns the list of permissions that this view requires.

get_queryset()[source]¶

Get the list of items for this view. This must be an iterable, and may be a queryset. Defaults to using self.queryset.

This method should always be used rather than accessing self.queryset directly, as self.queryset gets evaluated only once, and those results are cached for all subsequent requests.

You may want to override this if you need to provide different querysets depending on the incoming request.

(Eg. return a list of items that is specific to the user)

serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

class news.views.ArticleUpdateView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: JournalistOrEditorMixin, UpdateView

Allow an article’s author or any editor to edit an existing article.

fields = ['title', 'content', 'publisher']¶
get_queryset()[source]¶

Return the QuerySet that will be used to look up the object.

This method is called by the default implementation of get_object() and may not be called if get_object() is overridden.

model¶

alias of Article

success_url = '/'¶
template_name = 'news/article_form.html'¶
class news.views.EditorRequiredMixin[source]¶

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, UserPassesTestMixin

Restrict template views to Editor-role users.

test_func()[source]¶
class news.views.JournalistOrEditorMixin[source]¶

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, UserPassesTestMixin

Restrict template views to Journalist or Editor users.

test_func()[source]¶

Display the full contents of a single newsletter.

context_object_name = 'newsletter'¶
model¶

alias of Newsletter

template_name = 'news/newsletter_detail.html'¶
class news.views.NewsletterListCreateAPIView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ListCreateAPIView

GET /api/newsletters/ — list all newsletters. POST /api/newsletters/ — create a newsletter (journalists/editors).

get_permissions()[source]¶

Instantiates and returns the list of permissions that this view requires.

get_queryset()[source]¶

Get the list of items for this view. This must be an iterable, and may be a queryset. Defaults to using self.queryset.

This method should always be used rather than accessing self.queryset directly, as self.queryset gets evaluated only once, and those results are cached for all subsequent requests.

You may want to override this if you need to provide different querysets depending on the incoming request.

(Eg. return a list of items that is specific to the user)

serializer_class¶

alias of NewsletterSerializer

class news.views.NewsletterListView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, ListView

List all newsletters for authenticated users.

context_object_name = 'newsletters'¶
model¶

alias of Newsletter

queryset = <QuerySet []>¶
template_name = 'news/newsletter_list.html'¶
class news.views.NewsletterRetrieveUpdateDestroyAPIView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: RetrieveUpdateDestroyAPIView

GET/PUT/DELETE /api/newsletters/<id>/ — retrieve, update, or delete a newsletter.

get_permissions()[source]¶

Instantiates and returns the list of permissions that this view requires.

get_queryset()[source]¶

Get the list of items for this view. This must be an iterable, and may be a queryset. Defaults to using self.queryset.

This method should always be used rather than accessing self.queryset directly, as self.queryset gets evaluated only once, and those results are cached for all subsequent requests.

You may want to override this if you need to provide different querysets depending on the incoming request.

(Eg. return a list of items that is specific to the user)

serializer_class¶

alias of NewsletterSerializer

class news.views.NewsletterUpdateView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: JournalistOrEditorMixin, UpdateView

Allow the newsletter author or any editor to update a newsletter.

fields = ['title', 'description', 'articles']¶
get_queryset()[source]¶

Return the QuerySet that will be used to look up the object.

This method is called by the default implementation of get_object() and may not be called if get_object() is overridden.

model¶

alias of Newsletter

success_url = '/newsletters/'¶
template_name = 'news/newsletter_form.html'¶

GET /api/publishers/ — list all publishers.

alias of PublisherSerializer

class news.views.RegisterAPIView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: APIView

POST /api/register/ — create a new user account.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.AllowAny'>]¶
post(request)[source]¶
class news.views.SubscribedArticlesAPIView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ListAPIView

GET /api/articles/subscribed/ — articles from the reader’s subscribed publishers/journalists.

get_queryset()[source]¶

Get the list of items for this view. This must be an iterable, and may be a queryset. Defaults to using self.queryset.

This method should always be used rather than accessing self.queryset directly, as self.queryset gets evaluated only once, and those results are cached for all subsequent requests.

You may want to override this if you need to provide different querysets depending on the incoming request.

(Eg. return a list of items that is specific to the user)

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

class news.views.UserProfileAPIView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: RetrieveUpdateAPIView

GET/PUT /api/profile/ — view or update the authenticated user’s profile.

get_object()[source]¶

Returns the object the view is displaying.

You may want to override this if you need to provide non-standard queryset lookups. Eg if objects are referenced using multiple keyword arguments in the url conf.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
serializer_class¶

alias of UserSerializer

news.views.approve_article_view(request, pk)[source]¶

Approve a single article — Editor only. Option 2: notification logic handled directly in the view (no signals for approval).

news.views.login_view(request)[source]¶

Display the login form and authenticate the submitted credentials.

news.views.logout_view(request)[source]¶

Log out the current user and redirect to the login page.

news.views.register_view(request)[source]¶

Display the registration form and create a new user account on valid submission.

news.serializers¶

Serializers for the News Application REST API.

class news.serializers.ArticleApprovalSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Minimal serializer used by the editor approval endpoint.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

fields = ['approved']¶
model¶

alias of Article

class news.serializers.ArticleSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serialize Article instances; exposes author username and publisher name as read-only.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'title', 'content', 'author', 'author_username', 'created_at', 'updated_at', 'approved', 'approved_by', 'approved_at', 'publisher', 'publisher_name']¶
model¶

alias of Article

read_only_fields = ['author', 'created_at', 'updated_at', 'approved', 'approved_by', 'approved_at']¶
create(validated_data)[source]¶

We have a bit of extra checking around this in order to provide descriptive messages when something goes wrong, but this method is essentially just:

return ExampleModel.objects.create(**validated_data)

If there are many to many fields present on the instance then they cannot be set until the model is instantiated, in which case the implementation is like so:

example_relationship = validated_data.pop(‘example_relationship’) instance = ExampleModel.objects.create(**validated_data) instance.example_relationship = example_relationship return instance

The default implementation also does not handle nested relationships. If you want to support writable nested relationships you’ll need to write an explicit .create() method.

class news.serializers.NewsletterSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serialize Newsletter instances; accepts article_ids on write and returns full articles on read.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'title', 'description', 'created_at', 'updated_at', 'author', 'author_username', 'articles', 'article_ids']¶
model¶

alias of Newsletter

read_only_fields = ['author', 'created_at', 'updated_at']¶
create(validated_data)[source]¶

We have a bit of extra checking around this in order to provide descriptive messages when something goes wrong, but this method is essentially just:

return ExampleModel.objects.create(**validated_data)

If there are many to many fields present on the instance then they cannot be set until the model is instantiated, in which case the implementation is like so:

example_relationship = validated_data.pop(‘example_relationship’) instance = ExampleModel.objects.create(**validated_data) instance.example_relationship = example_relationship return instance

The default implementation also does not handle nested relationships. If you want to support writable nested relationships you’ll need to write an explicit .create() method.

class news.serializers.PublisherSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serialize Publisher instances to/from JSON.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'name', 'description', 'website', 'created_at']¶
model¶

alias of Publisher

read_only_fields = ['created_at']¶
class news.serializers.RegisterSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Handles new user registration.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

fields = ['username', 'email', 'password', 'password2', 'role', 'first_name', 'last_name']¶
model¶

alias of CustomUser

create(validated_data)[source]¶

Hash the password and create the user record.

validate(data)[source]¶

Ensure both password fields match before saving.

class news.serializers.UserSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializer for user profiles — hides sensitive fields.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'username', 'email', 'first_name', 'last_name', 'role', 'bio', 'publisher', 'subscribed_publishers', 'subscribed_journalists']¶
model¶

alias of CustomUser

read_only_fields = ['role']¶

news.permissions¶

class news.permissions.IsEditor[source]¶

Bases: BasePermission

Allow access only to users with the Editor role.

has_permission(request, view)[source]¶

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

class news.permissions.IsJournalist[source]¶

Bases: BasePermission

Allow access only to users with the Journalist role.

has_permission(request, view)[source]¶

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

class news.permissions.IsJournalistOrEditor[source]¶

Bases: BasePermission

Allow access to journalists and editors.

has_permission(request, view)[source]¶

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

class news.permissions.IsOwnerOrEditor[source]¶

Bases: BasePermission

Object-level: allow the article author or any editor to modify.

has_object_permission(request, view, obj)[source]¶

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

class news.permissions.IsReader[source]¶

Bases: BasePermission

Allow access only to users with the Reader role.

has_permission(request, view)[source]¶

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

news.services¶

Business-logic services for article approval side-effects:

  • Email subscribers of the article’s author/publisher.

  • Post a tweet to the configured X (Twitter) account.

news.services.notify_subscribers(article)[source]¶

Send an approval notification email to every subscriber of the article’s author (journalist) and the article’s publisher (if any).

news.services.post_to_x(article)[source]¶

Post a tweet announcing the newly approved article to the configured X account. Uses the X API v2 /tweets endpoint with OAuth 1.0a User Context.

news.forms¶

class news.forms.CustomUserCreationForm(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: UserCreationForm

Registration form that exposes the role field.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: Meta

fields = ('username', 'email', 'first_name', 'last_name', 'role', 'password1', 'password2')¶
model¶

alias of CustomUser

base_fields = {'email': <django.forms.fields.EmailField object>, 'first_name': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'last_name': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'role': <django.forms.fields.TypedChoiceField object>, 'username': <django.contrib.auth.forms.UsernameField object>}¶
declared_fields = {'email': <django.forms.fields.EmailField object>, 'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}¶
property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

save(commit=True)[source]¶

Save this form’s self.instance object if commit=True. Otherwise, add a save_m2m() method to the form which can be called after the instance is saved manually at a later time. Return the model instance.
